summary: RINA STRESS TEST AUDIT Complete - All 4 scenarios passed (28/28 tests). Fixed bug: POST /api/fpc/batches was not saving commessa_id field.
seed_data_creation: Test data created and cleaned up: preventivo, commessa, material batch (colata X12345), registro saldatura entry, DOP automatica
context_for_next_testing_agent: RINA stress test audit completed successfully. All 4 scenarios verified: (1) Preventivo→Commessa flow with EN_1090/EXC2, (2) Safety gate blocks approval when checks fail, (3) Material traceability with colata X12345 flows to Riesame and DOP, (4) DOP automatica collects all data correctly. Bug fixed: POST /api/fpc/batches was missing commessa_id field - now properly saves and links batches to commesse for traceability.

tests: strumenti_tarati reads real instrument expiry dates - OK
saldatori_qualificati reads real welder qualifications - OK
Colata X12345 appears in Riesame materiali_confermati - OK
DOP contains colata X12345 in batches_rintracciabilita - OK
All test data cleaned up successfully

bug_fixed.description: POST /api/fpc/batches was not saving commessa_id field
bug_fixed.root_cause: MaterialBatchCreate model in models/fpc.py was missing commessa_id field, and create_batch endpoint in routes/fpc.py was not including it in the document
fix_applied: Added commessa_id: Optional[str] = None to MaterialBatchCreate model
Added commessa_id to create_batch endpoint document
Added commessa_id handling to update_batch endpoint
bug_fixed.impact: Material batches can now be properly linked to commesse for EN 1090 traceability requirements

rca_of_the_issue: The POST /api/fpc/batches endpoint was accepting commessa_id in the request body but not saving it to the database. This broke the traceability chain required by EN 1090 - material batches need to be linked to specific commesse so that Riesame Tecnico can verify materials and DOP can include batch traceability data.
